tasks.title: Add RLS policies to agent tables
tasks.description: Using superpower skills. Add PostgreSQL Row-Level Security policies to the 6 agent tables via SQL migration. Each firm must only see its own data at the database level.
tasks.context: Prisma does NOT support @@policy directives — use a raw SQL migration file in prisma/migrations/. Follow the existing pattern from 001_rls/migration.sql which uses `get_current_firm_id()` session variable. The 6 tables (EmailIngressConfig, IncomingEmail, EmailAttachment, AgentAction, AgentActionModification, AgentMetricsDaily) all have firmId field mapped to firm_id column in DB. Application-level filtering already exists in all API routes.
steps: Read prisma/migrations/001_rls/migration.sql to understand the existing RLS pattern
Enable RLS on all 6 tables: ALTER TABLE ... ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY
Create policy using USING (firm_id = get_current_firm_id()) WITH CHECK (firm_id = get_current_firm_id())
Run `npx vitest run` to verify no regressions
criteria: Migration SQL file exists with RLS policies for all 6 agent tables
Each table has policy using get_current_firm_id() for SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
`npx prisma validate` passes without errors
`npx vitest run` shows 0 new failures

tasks.title: Add rate limiting to agent API endpoints
tasks.description: Using superpower skills. Apply the existing rate limiter to all agent API routes that don't already have it.
tasks.context: Rate limiter at src/lib/rate-limit.ts exports `applyRateLimit(group, firmId, max, windowMs)` which returns `{ response, headers }`. Usage pattern: `const rl = await applyRateLimit('group', user.firmId, 60, 60_000); if (rl.response) return rl.response;`. Agent endpoints are under src/app/api/agent/ with subdirs: actions (8 routes), config (4 routes), emails (2 routes), events, health, ingest, learning, metrics, oauth (4 routes). Ingest already uses checkRateLimit — do NOT double-apply. Limits: ingest 100/min per firm, actions 60/min per user, config/test 5/min per user.
steps: Read src/lib/rate-limit.ts to understand applyRateLimit API
Grep all route.ts under src/app/api/agent/ for existing rate limit calls
Add applyRateLimit to routes that don't have it
Run `npx vitest run` — if rate limiter state causes test failures, mock applyRateLimit in affected test files
criteria: Every agent API route file has rate limiting (applyRateLimit or checkRateLimit)
`npx vitest run` shows 0 new failures
No duplicate rate limiting on ingest endpoint

tasks.title: Create agent action detail page
tasks.description: Using superpower skills. Create a standalone page at /agent/actions/[id] showing full details of a single agent action.
tasks.context: Dashboard (src/app/agent/page.tsx, 718 lines) has inline action cards as reference. API: GET /api/agent/actions/[id] returns action with email data. Layout at src/app/agent/layout.tsx. Page needs: email preview (HTML/text toggle), extracted data fields (old → new), action controls (confirm/modify/reject), audit trail timeline.
steps: Read src/app/agent/page.tsx to understand existing action card UI and data fetching patterns
Read src/app/api/agent/actions/[id]/route.ts to understand API response shape
Run `npx vitest run` to verify no regressions
criteria: File src/app/agent/actions/[id]/page.tsx exists with >100 lines
Page renders: type, status, confidence, changes, email content, confirm/modify/reject controls, audit trail
`npx tsc --noEmit` has 0 errors from new file
`npx vitest run` shows 0 new failures

tasks.title: Implement agent notification system
tasks.description: Using superpower skills. Build notification system: daily digest email + urgent notifications for high-priority items.
tasks.context: Existing renewal notifications at src/services/notification-service.ts (reference pattern). EmailService available for sending. Worker at src/worker/agent-worker.ts runs background jobs. Pipeline at src/services/agent/pipeline.ts processes emails. Create src/services/agent/notifications.ts with: sendDailyDigest(firmId) for daily summary, sendUrgentNotification(firmId, action) for claims/cancellations/low-confidence. Hook into worker at appropriate points. Digest content: processed email count, pending actions summary, accuracy stats.
steps: Create src/services/agent/notifications.ts with digest + urgent functions
criteria: Notification module exports sendDailyDigest and sendUrgentNotification
Digest includes: processed email count, pending actions, accuracy stats
Urgent triggers on: claims, cancellations, confidence < 0.5
Worker calls notification functions
`npx vitest run` shows 0 new failures

tasks.title: Write end-to-end tests for agent pipeline
tasks.description: Using superpower skills. Write 5 E2E test scenarios exercising the agent pipeline from email ingestion to action creation.
tasks.context: Test files in src/__tests__/unit/. Use vitest with mocked prisma and external services. Pipeline: ingest → classify → extract → match → generate action. Reference existing test mocks in other test files.
steps: Read existing test files for mock patterns
criteria: File src/__tests__/unit/agent-e2e.test.ts exists
5 scenarios: renewal → update, new policy → create, non-insurance → skip, duplicate → dedupe, confirm → audit
All 5 tests pass: `npx vitest run src/__tests__/unit/agent-e2e.test.ts`
`npx vitest run` overall shows 0 new failures

tasks.title: Write PII integration test
tasks.description: Using superpower skills. Write integration tests verifying PII desensitization end-to-end: real PII not in LLM requests, correctly restored in output.
tasks.context: PII module at src/lib/agent/pii.ts (173 lines). Functions: desensitizePII(text) → { sanitized, mapping }, resensitize(text, mapping) → original. PII types: email, phone (Irish 085/+353), policy number, PPS, DOB.
steps: Read existing PII unit tests for test patterns
criteria: File src/__tests__/unit/agent-pii-integration.test.ts exists
Test: desensitize → resensitize round-trip restores all PII
Test: desensitized text contains zero original PII values
Covers ≥3 PII types (email, phone, policy number)
Edge cases: nested PII, no PII, unicode
`npx vitest run` shows 0 new failures

tasks.title: Write load test for email processing
tasks.description: Using superpower skills. Write a load test simulating 100 emails through the agent pipeline. Measure throughput, latency, error rate.
tasks.context: Pipeline at src/services/agent/pipeline.ts. Mock prisma and LLM calls. Generate 100 realistic insurance emails (renewal, new policy, claim, cancellation, etc). Process in batches. Assert: error rate < 5%, test < 60s.
steps: Generate 100 mock emails with varied insurance content
Process all through pipeline, measure performance
criteria: File src/__tests__/unit/agent-load.test.ts exists
100 mock emails processed
Reports: total duration, emails/sec, error count
Error rate < 5%, test completes < 60s
`npx vitest run` shows 0 new failures
